key_insight: A layerwise self-adaptive PINN with dual-phase optimization better captures sharp shock fronts in high-Reynolds-number multidimensional coupled Burgers' equations than standard PINNs.
engineering_relevance: This offers a more stable, mesh-free surrogate for resolving steep gradient flows where finite difference and finite element methods struggle with stability and mesh dependence.
method: Dynamically reweights physics residual, initial condition, and boundary condition losses during training.
Uses a dual-phase optimization strategy for more stable convergence.
Implemented in MATLAB and tested against standard PINN baselines with and without L-BFGS.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical experiments showing lower relative L2 error and improved shock-front capture compared with standard PINN variants.
watch_item: Performance may depend on careful tuning of adaptive weights and optimizer phases, especially for very stiff, high-Re flows.
paper_title: MATLAB-Based Layerwise Self-Adaptive Physics-Informed Neural Network in Applications to Multidimensional Coupled Burgers' Equations with High Reynolds Numbers
